cross_eval_summary.pattern: Skill package passes all technical expectations (100% pass rate) but loses 100% of pairwise comparisons
cross_eval_summary.key_insight: The interaction protocol (staged execution with pause blocks) creates friction that outweighs structural correctness benefits
cross_eval_summary.critical_finding: Technical compliance does not equal user preference - judges consistently prefer direct, complete answers over structured staged interaction

repair_recommendations.action: Redesign interaction protocol to be adaptive rather than mandatory staged execution
repair_recommendations.details: Add early detection of user preference (direct vs. staged) in Step 0; make pause blocks optional based on user signals; allow complete output when information is already sufficient

repair_recommendations.action: Enhance output quality to justify the structured approach
repair_recommendations.details: When using staged interaction, ensure each step provides substantial standalone value; add quick-win insights in clarification requests so users get immediate value

repair_recommendations.action: Revise output contract to balance structure with actionability
repair_recommendations.details: Reduce emphasis on format compliance markers (pause blocks, step labels) when they conflict with user experience; prioritize clear recommendations over structural perfection
